ip_outcome_note: Card name states 'Announcer // Announcer, Referee Veteran'. Suspected IP was 'Tournament Announcer (Dragon Ball)' at medium confidence. Art depicts a blonde suited figure with a large microphone in an arena — fully consistent with the World Martial Arts Tournament Announcer's canonical appearance. Character is unnamed in canon but is a recurring figure across Dragon Ball, DBZ, and GT. IP verified as the unnamed WMA Tournament Announcer; no external IP risk.

trivia_bullets.text: The World Martial Arts Tournament Announcer is a nameless recurring character who appears across Dragon Ball, Dragon Ball Z, and Dragon Ball GT — one of the few non-combatant faces present at nearly every major tournament in the franchise. His job is entirely service-labor: narrating the spectacle for an audience while the combatants receive glory. [Dragon Ball Wiki: World Tournament Announcer, via WebSearch snippet]

This card's double-sided format ('Announcer // Announcer, Referee Veteran') is a DBS card game mechanic for leader or battle cards with two states — in this case, the Announcer's role escalating to referee-veteran status within the set's tournament framing. It is the only card in DBS-TB02 to canonize this nameless recurring character as a playable unit. [dbs-cardgame.com card list, via WebSearch snippet]

Community sentiment: the Announcer is a long-running fan-favorite background character precisely because of his longevity and anonymity — he has witnessed every major tournament since the original Dragon Ball without ever earning a canonical name. His card inclusion in TB2 is widely noted as a 'deep cut' pull for fans who appreciate series continuity. [Behind the Voice Actors: World Martial Arts Tournament Announcer, via WebSearch snippet]

TB2-065 is an Uncommon in DBS-TB02, the highest-number card in the set (set runs to TB2-069), placing him at the tail end of the print run — structurally appropriate for a figure who always appears at the end of the event, not the beginning. [dbs-cardgame.com; bleedingcool.com: TB02 checklist]

related_cards.name: Master Shen, Martial Meister
related_cards.note: Shen's match-fixing at the 22nd Tournament was the kind of event the Announcer would have narrated without knowing the full corruption happening on the floor.

related_cards.name: Unyielding Victory Jackie Chun
related_cards.note: The Announcer called Jackie Chun's matches without knowing he was Master Roshi — canonical witness to the disguise from the outside.
